授業目的: This course offers a general introduction to the major historial, political, and social trends of contemporary Japan that affect the future of Japan, East Asia, and the world including the United States. Unlike the courses that merely focus on economic development, political institutions, and elite politics, this course focuses on the response of businesses and governments to the globalized world. Upon successful completion of this course, students will be able to: (1) understand contemporary history of Japanese politics, society, economy, business, and culture since World War II; (2) analyze continuity, change, and challenges in various aspects of Japanese politics and society; and (3) critcally evaluate the responses of the Japanese businesses, governments, and various actors in Japanese politics and society to the major challenges facing Japan. No prior knowledge of Japanese politics or history will be assumed.
到達目標: (1) Understand contemporary history of Japanese politics, society, economy, business, and culture since World War II; (2) Analyze continuity, change,and challenges in various aspects of Japanese politics and society; (3) Critically evaluate the responses of the Japanese businesses, governments, and various actors in Japanese politics and society to the major challenges facing Japan.
授業の概要・背景: The course is designed, and will be conducted, as a seminar. How well this course achieves its goals will depend on students' participation. Students are expected to do the assigned reading before class; to have read actively, comparing what they are reading with what they already know through other readings in this class or other classes to join class discussion.

第2回: Domestic Politics and International Relations of Japan
第6回: Global Value Chains in the Asia-Pacific
第14回: Japanese Political Economy of the Digital Transformation
第15回: Japanese Political Economy of the Pandemic
